# opnsense_quagga_bgp_global (Resource)

Manages BGP global configuration on OPNsense (FRR). Singleton resource — one BGP config per appliance; `terraform destroy` only removes it from state. Requires the `os-frr` plugin and an enabled `opnsense_quagga_general`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "opnsense_quagga_bgp_global" "this" {
  enabled              = true
  as_number            = 65010
  router_id            = "10.0.0.1"
  networks             = ["10.0.0.0/24", "10.0.1.0/24"]
  log_neighbor_changes = true

  depends_on = [opnsense_quagga_general.this]
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `as_number` (Number) Local autonomous system number (1-4294967295).

### Optional

- `distance` (Number) Administrative distance (1-255, 0 = unset).
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable BGP. Defaults to `false`.
- `enforce_first_as` (Boolean) Enforce first AS in AS-path of eBGP updates. Defaults to `true`.
- `graceful_restart` (Boolean) Enable graceful restart. Defaults to `false`.
- `log_neighbor_changes` (Boolean) Log neighbor up/down changes. Defaults to `false`.
- `maximum_paths` (Number) Maximum eBGP ECMP paths (1-128, 0 = unset).
- `maximum_paths_ibgp` (Number) Maximum iBGP ECMP paths (1-128, 0 = unset).
- `network_import_check` (Boolean) Check BGP network route existence in IGP. Defaults to `true`.
- `networks` (Set of String) Networks (CIDR) to advertise into BGP.
- `router_id` (String) BGP router ID (IPv4 address). Empty = derived automatically.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) Synthetic identifier for this singleton (always `bgp`).
